frameworks.name: predictive coding
frameworks.one_line: visual cortex codes prediction error between expected and observed object features

frameworks.name: object-based prediction error mixing
frameworks.one_line: surprise on one feature spreads to expected features within the same object

frameworks.name: feature-independent prediction
frameworks.one_line: each feature's expectation processed independently of other features

frameworks.name: object segmentation by surprise
frameworks.one_line: mismatched features inferred to belong to different objects

core_question: How does an unexpected feature of an object affect concurrent processing of another, expected feature within the same object?
key_assumptions: visual cognition relies on contextual expectations
expected and unexpected stimuli yield distinct neural signatures
features of a familiar object can carry separable expectations
manipulations: independent expectations for color of moving-dot stimuli
independent expectations for motion direction of moving-dot stimuli
factorial pairing of expected/unexpected feature combinations
key_findings: behavior and visual cortex signals showed mixing of prediction error across features
surprise in one feature spread to the concurrently expected feature
results favored object-wide unexpectedness over feature-independent or object-splitting accounts
interpretations: prediction error is shared across features of a single object
an unexpected feature renders the whole object unexpected at the neural level
